name: Makiki / Tantalus
desc: Established residential hillside climbing toward Tantalus — mature trees, mid-range homes, scenic loop drive, and trailheads galore.
sources: Beat of Hawaii · Forum locals · AlohaTonya
insider: Makiki Valley Loop trail (Maunalaha-Makiki Valley-Kanealole) is a 2.5-mile loop way less crowded than Manoa Falls
Tantalus Drive / Round Top Drive loop is the best scenic drive on the island — lush canopy, zero tourists
The Honolulu Museum of Art on Beretania (the main branch) has an outstanding collection and a peaceful courtyard cafe — free first-Wednesday and first-Friday admission
Round Table Pizza on Wilder has been the no-frills neighborhood hangout for decades — cheap pitchers
The Tantalus trailhead network connects to over 10 different trails — you could hike a new route every weekend for months
Wild pigs, mongoose, and feral cats roam the upper Tantalus area — don't leave food out and secure trash cans
Night driving on Tantalus is sketchy — no streetlights, sharp curves, and occasional car break-ins at pulloffs after dark
Homes on Tantalus proper sit in genuine cloud forest — gorgeous but perpetually damp with serious termite pressure
Makiki Heights Drive has some of the most architecturally interesting mid-century homes in Honolulu
The Moleka trailhead parking lot fills by 8am on weekends — arrive early or park along the road below
